title: Vegetable Sambar with Rice
description: Tangy toor dal sambar with everyday vegetables and rice—a comforting South Indian plant-based main.
explanation: Use whichever non-root seasonal vegetables are available. Tamarind, sambar powder, and a mustard-curry-leaf tempering make a modest pot of dal feel complete.
ingredients: 3 cups Mixed Vegetables (drumstick, pumpkin, beans, brinjal, tomato, or okra)
instructions: Cook rice and keep warm. Pressure-cook or boil toor dal with turmeric until soft; mash lightly.
Simmer vegetables in water with tomato and salt until nearly tender.
Add cooked dal, tamarind pulp, and sambar powder. Simmer 8–10 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the broth tastes rounded.
Heat oil, crackle mustard seeds, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the sambar.
Finish with coriander and serve hot with rice.
